description:
Goal: derive backend-ready ABI and emission facts from materialized closed artifacts without reopening semantic discovery. Signal: interpreter, JIT, and AOT adapters consume a settled, change-honest materialized program and never rediscover reachability, dispatch, return lanes, or type flow.

Blocked on the fixpoint cleanup arc: backend work resumes only after activation inputs are modeled as joined caller contributions, semantic closure is read-only, the fact table has value fingerprints, runtime bootstrap is lazy, and docs describe the corrected contract.

Change:
- Add AbiReady and Emitted facts for interpreter, JIT, and AOT adapters from the materialized program.
- Codegen consumes callable entries, return contracts, effects, extern marshal facts, and allocation-relevant facts handed over by materialization.
- Backend adapters report unsupported or missing closed facts as fatal artifact errors; telemetry carries raw ids/facts, not formatted summaries.

Why:
- Codegen should lower settled facts, not decide reachability, dispatch, return lanes, or type flow.

Checks:
- Backend attempts to ask type/callee/reachability questions are fatal.
- Quicksort and Enum.reduce preserve the expected absence/presence and allocation contracts through telemetry plus runtime/artifact assertions.
title: Compiler2 artifacts: derive ABI and emission from closed facts
